commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is a narrow repair: extend the malformed plugin registry guard to the node-command allowlist path and add one gateway-level regression.; Needs attention: The diff touches a node.invoke security boundary and leaves the real gateway path partially unguarded before the new policy result can be returned.; Review finding: [P2] Guard the real node.invoke entrypoint — src/gateway/node-invoke-plugin-policy.ts:3... (sha=43a58dd49c68fb23d478551352c149234fcea64e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens gateway node.invoke plugin policy handling for unreadable policy and dangerous-command registrations and adds focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +261, Tests +142. Total +403 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a malformed registry row can be read by resolveNodeCommandAllowlist before applyPluginNodeInvokePolicy runs. I did not execute the gateway repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow repair: extend the malformed plugin registry guard to the node-command allowlist path and add one gateway-level regression.
